tool_name: Multi-Product Break-Even Analysis and Degree of Operating Leverage (DOL)

inputs.name: Total Annual Fixed Costs (FC)
inputs.hint: Rent, salaries, depreciation, insurance — costs that do not vary with output
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Target Net Profit After Tax
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Corporate Income Tax Rate
inputs.hint: Turkey: 25% | EU average: 21% | US federal: 21% + state

inputs.name: Product A — Selling Price
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Product A — Variable Cost per Unit
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Product B — Selling Price
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Product B — Variable Cost per Unit
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Semi-Variable (Step) Costs
inputs.hint: Costs that step up at volume thresholds — overtime, extra shifts
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

formulas: CM_A = product_A_price - product_A_vc   // [USD/unit] Contribution Margin — Product A
CM_B = product_B_price - product_B_vc   // [USD/unit] Contribution Margin — Product B
Mix_Sum = Mix_A + Mix_B   // [—] Validation: must equal 1.0
WACM = (CM_A * Mix_A + CM_B * Mix_B) / Mix_Sum   // [USD/unit] Weighted Average Contribution Margin
Total_FC_Adjusted = total_fixed_costs_usd + semi_variable_costs_usd   // [USD] Adjusted fixed cost base
BEP_Units = Total_FC_Adjusted / WACM   // [units] Break-Even Point in total units
BEP_Units_A = BEP_Units * Mix_A   // [units] BEP units for Product A
BEP_Units_B = BEP_Units * Mix_B   // [units] BEP units for Product B
BEP_Revenue = BEP_Units_A * product_A_price + BEP_Units_B * product_B_price   // [USD] Break-Even Revenue
Required_Pre_Tax_Profit = target_net_profit_usd / (1 - corporate_tax_rate_pct / 100)   // [USD] Pre-tax profit needed
Target_Volume_Units = (Total_FC_Adjusted + Required_Pre_Tax_Profit) / WACM   // [units] Volume required to hit profit target
Actual_Operating_Income = actual_total_volume * WACM - Total_FC_Adjusted   // [USD] EBIT at actual volume
Actual_Net_Profit = Actual_Operating_Income * (1 - corporate_tax_rate_pct / 100)   // [USD] Net income after tax
Margin_of_Safety_Units = actual_total_volume - BEP_Units   // [units] Safety cushion above BEP
Margin_of_Safety_Pct = (Margin_of_Safety_Units / actual_total_volume) * 100   // [%] MoS as % of actual volume
DOL = Actual_Operating_Income > 0 ? (actual_total_volume * WACM) / Actual_Operating_Income : 0   // [—] Degree of Operating Leverage — sensitivity multiplier

mix_sum_check.error_msg: Product mix percentages must sum to 100% (±2% tolerance). Current sum is invalid — WACM calculation will be distorted.

negative_cm_check.error_msg: Weighted Average Contribution Margin ≤ 0: Variable costs exceed selling prices on a weighted basis. Break-even is mathematically unreachable — this product mix destroys value at every unit sold.

negative_cm_product.error_msg: At least one product has negative contribution margin (price < variable cost). Selling more units increases losses. Price revision or discontinuation must be evaluated immediately.

tax_rate_range.error_msg: Tax rate cannot equal or exceed 100%. Net profit would be zero or negative by definition.

smart_warnings.message: EXTREME OPERATING LEVERAGE (DOL > 5): A 10% revenue decline will reduce operating income by more than 50%. Fixed cost structure is dangerously high relative to volume. Immediate fixed cost reduction or revenue diversification required.
High Operating Leverage (DOL 3–5): Business is highly sensitive to volume fluctuations. In an economic downturn, profitability will erode rapidly. Stress-test at -15% and -30% volume scenarios.

CRITICAL: Margin of Safety < 10%. Actual sales are within 10% of break-even. Any demand reduction, price pressure, or cost increase could push the business into loss. Pricing, cost structure, or volume must be addressed immediately.
Low Margin of Safety (10–25%): Business has limited buffer above break-even. Acceptable for stable industries; concerning for cyclical manufacturing or export-dependent operations.

Contribution Margin Ratio < 20% on one or more products: Very thin margin structure. Small cost increases (raw materials, energy) will immediately eliminate profitability. Price elasticity analysis required.
Break-even revenue exceeds $10M: High absolute break-even level. Ensure sales pipeline and contracted revenue cover this threshold before capacity expansion.

fmea.effect: Product mix percentages must sum to 100% (±2% tolerance). Current sum is invalid — WACM calculation will be distorted.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Weighted Average Contribution Margin ≤ 0: Variable costs exceed selling prices on a weighted basis. Break-even is mathematically unreachable — this product mix destroys value at every unit sold.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: At least one product has negative contribution margin (price < variable cost). Selling more units increases losses. Price revision or discontinuation must be evaluated immediately.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Tax rate cannot equal or exceed 100%. Net profit would be zero or negative by definition.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: EXTREME OPERATING LEVERAGE (DOL > 5): A 10% revenue decline will reduce operating income by more than 50%. Fixed cost structure is dangerously high relative to volume. Immediate fixed cost reduction or revenue diversification required.
High Operating Leverage (DOL 3–5): Business is highly sensitive to volume fluctuations. In an economic downturn, profitability will erode rapidly. Stress-test at -15% and -30% volume scenarios.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: DOL > 3 && DOL <= 5

fmea.failureMode: CRITICAL: Margin of Safety < 10%. Actual sales are within 10% of break-even. Any demand reduction, price pressure, or cost increase could push the business into loss. Pricing, cost structure, or volume must be addressed immediately.
Low Margin of Safety (10–25%): Business has limited buffer above break-even. Acceptable for stable industries; concerning for cyclical manufacturing or export-dependent operations.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Margin_of_Safety_Pct >= 10 && Margin_of_Safety_Pct < 25

fmea.failureMode: Contribution Margin Ratio < 20% on one or more products: Very thin margin structure. Small cost increases (raw materials, energy) will immediately eliminate profitability. Price elasticity analysis required.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: CM_A / product_A_price < 0.20 || CM_B / product_B_price < 0.20

fmea.failureMode: Break-even revenue exceeds $10M: High absolute break-even level. Ensure sales pipeline and contracted revenue cover this threshold before capacity expansion.
